what_it_does: Defines register(mcp, suite), which attaches two MCP tools—project and layers—to an MCP server. project exposes the same radial projection resolver used by the visual surface, accepting lens, space, embedder, MRL dimension, scale rung, relative centre, time instant, separator poles, and limit; it returns the projection body or, when no binding is given, the list of available bindings. layers returns the per-space embedder-layer dimensions so callers can choose emb and bound dim.
observations: Heavy docstring frames the file as 'the instrument's tool face' and the MCP twin of a visual surface.
register is the single entry point that decorates two nested tool functions with @mcp.tool().
project delegates to suite.project with a query dictionary built from provided parameters.
When binding is empty, project calls suite.project with binding=raw and limit=1 to harvest the registry-true bindings list.
All parameter values are passed as strings in the query dict, including limit and dim.
Non-200 responses have _status injected into the returned body to make failures explicit.
layers simply returns suite.layer_dims(), exposing available embedder layers and full dimensions per space.
The design goal is reuse-don't-parallel: the MCP tools call the same resolver the UI uses.
standouts: The file treats MCP tools as a first-class 'face' of the instrument, not an afterthought, with explicit UI-MCP parity ('Tim's law').
project's parameter surface directly mirrors UI controls: lens, layer, resolution, scale rung, relative centre, time scrubber, and separator poles.
Registry-true / parametric intent: new bindings, spaces, and embedder layers should become drivable without editing this file.
conventions: Docstring-driven API documentation; each tool's docstring is long and semantic.
Nested function definitions inside register, relying on closure over suite.
Proxy pattern: tools are thin wrappers around suite methods.
Fail-loud: errors and non-200 statuses are returned in the response body.
concerns: When binding is empty, it still performs a projection call (raw, limit=1) only to extract metadata; a dedicated bindings endpoint would be cleaner.
Parameter coercion to strings is inconsistent with the declared int types (dim, limit), which may confuse callers or tests.
No local validation of binding names, address formats, or dim bounds; all validation is delegated to suite.project.
_status is added only on non-200, so success/failure shape differs slightly.
notes: This is the MCP door onto the radial projection engine: two thin, self-describing tools that keep the agent and the UI on the same resolver.
questions: What contract does suite.project expect for addresses in center, pole_a, and pole_b?
How does the suite detect and report an embedder-dimension mismatch?
What is the exact structure of the projection body and the nucleation/separation reports?
What spaces and embedder layers are actually registered at runtime?
How is the visual surface's bridge.build_projection related to suite.project?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it registers MCP tools that proxy to the same projection and layer-discovery resolver the UI uses, with registry-true parameter discovery.
apparent_themes: UI-MCP symmetry as a design law
Radial / semantic projection as a universal interface
Multi-layer embedding model with MRL resolution and scale rungs
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the agent interface a complete, discoverable, and behaviorally identical twin of the operator's visual controls, so that any new lens, space, or embedder layer automatically becomes drivable through MCP without a code change.
novelty: The explicit 'two faces of one engine' framing and the mapping of rich UI axes (MRL dimension, scale rung, temporal scrubber, relative centre, separator poles) onto a single MCP tool parameter surface.
contribution: A reusable pattern for exposing a projection engine as self-describing MCP tools; it keeps the agent and UI on the same resolver, avoiding a parallel projector, and makes new bindings/spaces/layers reachable through parameter discovery.
summary_for_embedding: Defines the MCP tool face of a radial projection instrument, registering project and layers tools that proxy to suite.project and suite.layer_dims with registry-true, UI-symmetric parameter discovery.
intention_for_embedding: To give agents the same discoverable, parametric access to the radial projection engine that operators have through the visual surface.

symbols.does: Attaches the project and layers MCP tools to the given mcp server using the provided suite.
symbols.description: The module's single public entry point. It closes over suite and registers two nested tool functions with @mcp.tool(), wiring MCP calls into the suite's projection and layer-discovery methods.

symbols.does: Resolves a lens/binding into a radial projection by building a query and calling suite.project.
symbols.description: Accepts the full drivable axes (binding, space, emb, dim, rung, center, at, types_space, pole_a, pole_b, limit). If binding is empty it calls suite.project with raw/limit=1 to return the available bindings list. Otherwise it forwards the query, injects _status on non-200, and returns the projection body.

symbols.does: Returns the per-space embedder-layer dimension map by calling suite.layer_dims().
symbols.description: The MCP twin of the UI layer picker and dimension source. It exposes which embedder layers exist for each space and their full dimensions, enabling callers to choose emb and bound dim without hard-coding layer metadata.
